title: Jerry Oltion

'''Jerry Oltion''' (born [[1950s|1957]])<ref name="SF Encylopedia" /> is an author and amateur astronomer. For ''[[Star Wars]]'' publishing, he wrote ''[[At the Crossroads: The Spacer's Tale]]'', a short story that appeared in the [[1995]] anthology ''[[Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina]]'', published by [[Bantam Spectra]].

In [[2011]], Oltion's eighty-fourth story was published in [[Wikipedia:Analog Science Fiction and Fact|''Analog'' magazine]], setting the record for the most fiction contributions in the magazine's history. He has also written various original science fiction novels and novellas, and his [[1997]] novella ''Abandon in Place'' won the [[Wikipedia:Nebula Award for Best Novella|Nebula Award for Best Novella]]. Oltion is additionally the author of four [[Wikipedia:List of Star Trek novels|''Star Trek'' novels]], and he has written various other tie-in fiction under the pen name '''Ryan Hughes'''.<ref name="Official Site" />

As an amateur astronomer, Oltion is the designer of the trackball telescope, which was featured in a [[2006]] issue of ''[[Wikipedia:Sky & Telescope|Sky & Telescope]]'' magazine. Oltion served as a regular columnist for the magazine from [[2016]] to [[2025]], writing about homemade astronomy equipment.<ref name="Official Site" />
